expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: context intake preserves component-stack constraints, embedding lifecycle assumptions, and integration-sensitive customer expectations
expectations.evidence: The output explicitly enumerates and evaluates all three focus areas under “Case focus coverage: preservation of critical context during intake”: “1) Component-stack constraints — preserved (contract-level)”, “2) Embedding lifecycle assumptions — partially covered”, and “3) Integration-sensitive customer expectations — covered…”. It also restates the focus in the Scope section: “Focus: context intake preserves (1) component-stack constraints, (2) embedding lifecycle assumptions, and (3) integration-sensitive customer expectations”.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase1
expectations.evidence: The output is explicitly Phase 1-scoped and discusses Phase 1 entry/work/output/post-validation: “Phase 1 contract alignment (what Phase 1 must do)” and “This is consistent with a Phase 1 = context intake & routing checkpoint; it does not draft QA scenarios”. It also states: “Output intentionally stays Phase 1-scoped (context intake/spawn + validation) per the phase model in the skill snapshot.”
